texts.full_text: r the femoral head and acetabulum, usually presents from the late childhood to early adulthood period with symptoms such as hip pain, limping, or knee discomfort. In cases where bilateral total hip arthroplasty (THA) is considered necessary due to advanced joint degeneration in patients with MED, several factors must be taken into account. These include the patient's age, bone quality, and overall health status, alongside specific anatomical considerations related to MED that can influence surgical planning. Surgical challenges for THA in these cases may arise from altered anatomy secondary to epiphyseal dysplasia affecting both femoral head coverage and acetabular morphology. Surgeons must be prepared to address potential issues such as: - Altered acetabular version - Reduced bone stock due to osteopenia or previous surgeries - Abnormal neck-shaft angles in the femur Preoperative planning with detailed imaging studies, including CT scans for 3D modeling of joint anatomy, can help tailor implant selection and surgical technique. Intraoperatively, techniques such as custom jigs may be necessary to achieve proper acetabular positioning despite altered bony landmarks. Post-operative care focuses on optimizing pain management while encouraging early mobilization within safe limits to prevent complications like deep vein thrombosis or infect

This was circumstantial in nature inasmuch as appellant denied committing the homicide, no murder weapon was ever found or traced to appellant, and there were no eyewitnesses.
